headings.text: How much do police station representatives earn?
Do police station reps get paid for travel?
Are police station reps employed or self-employed?
Do reps get paid more for night/weekend work?
When did fee rates last increase?
Need a Police Station Rep in Kent?

content: HomeResourcesPolice Station Rep PayLast Updated: 2nd February 2026Police Station Representative Pay RatesComplete guide to Legal Aid fee rates for police station representatives in England and Wales. Rates updated 22nd December 2025 per SI 2025/1251.ContentsHow Police Station Pay WorksLegal Aid Fee Rates (December 2025)Escape Fee CasesRealistic Earnings PotentialFrequently Asked QuestionsReferencesImportant: How Reps Get PaidPolice station representatives typically work as freelancers for criminal defence firms. The firm claims the Legal Aid fee and pays the representative an agreed rate (often a percentage of the fixed fee or an hourly rate). Rates vary between firms and by experience level.How Police Station Pay WorksPolice station work in England and Wales is primarily funded through Legal Aid.[3]The Legal Aid Agency sets fixed fees for police station attendance, which are paid to the solicitor's firm holding a Crime Contract.[4]Representatives are paid by the firm they work for - either as employees or freelancers. Payment arrangements vary, but common models include:A percentage of the Legal Aid fixed fee (typically 50-70%)An hourly rate (typically £20-35 per hour depending on experience)A flat rate per attendanceLegal Aid Fee Rates (December 2025)Fee CategoryRateNotesPolice Station Fixed Fee (all areas)£320.00Harmonised rate from 22 Dec 2025Escape Fee Threshold£650.00Unified threshold for hourly billingMileage Rate45p/mileFirst 10,000 miles per annumMileage (over 10,000)25p/mileAfter 10,000 miles per annumSource: The Criminal Legal Aid (General and Remuneration) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 (SI 2025/1251). Rates effective 22nd December 2025. Always verify current rates with the Legal Aid Agency.Escape Fee CasesIf a case requires significantly more work than average, it may qualify for "escape fee" payment - where the firm can claim hourly rates instead of the fixed fee.Escape fee cases require the time spent to exceed the fixed fee threshold (calculated at the hourly rate). Complex cases, long interviews, or multiple attendances can trigger escape fees.Use Escape Fee CalculatorRealistic Earnings PotentialEarnings as a police station representative vary significantly based on:Location: London and busy urban areas offer more workAvailability: Reps working unsocial hours (nights, weekends) get more casesReputation: Experienced reps who firms trust get more referralsNumber of firms: Working for multiple firms increases opportunitiesA full-time, busy representative might handle 10-20+ cases per week. Part-time or weekend-only reps might do 2-5 cases per week. Many representatives combine police station work with other legal or non-legal employment.Frequently Asked QuestionsHow much do police station representatives earn?Earnings vary significantly. The Legal Aid fixed fee is £320.00 per case (from Dec 2025), but representatives typically receive a portion of this (often £100-£180 per case) or an hourly rate. Full-time reps handling 10-15 cases per week could earn £40,000-60,000+ annually, while part-time reps earn proportionally less.Do police station reps get paid for travel?Yes, travel costs are reimbursed separately from the fixed fee. Mileage is typically paid at 45p per mile for the first 10,000 miles, dropping to 25p thereafter. Travel and waiting time may also be claimable in certain circumstances.Are police station reps employed or self-employed?Most representatives work as self-employed freelancers, though some firms employ reps directly. Freelance reps invoice firms for each attendance. Tax and National Insurance are the representative's responsibility.Do reps get paid more for night/weekend work?The Legal Aid fixed fee is the same regardless of when work is done. However, reps willing to work unsocial hours are more valuable to firms and may negotiate better rates or simply get more work than those only available 9-5.When did fee rates last increase?The most recent fee increase was on 22nd December 2025 under SI 2025/1251, which introduced harmonised rates of £320 (fixed fee) and £650 (escape threshold) across all 245 schemes. This replaced the previous varied scheme-based rates that ranged from £223.52 to £315.86.Interested in Police Station Work?Learn how to become an accredited representative and start earning in criminal defence.How to Become a Rep View Full Fee ScheduleReferencesThe Criminal Legal Aid (General and Remuneration) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 (SI 2025/1251) - legislation.gov.ukHMRC Approved Mileage Allowance Payments (AMAPs) - GOV.UKLegal Aid Agency - GOV.UK2025 Standard Crime Contract Specification - GOV.UKArticle Ref: PSRPAY-20251125-A1B2C3D4 Written by Robert Cashman, Court and Police Station Duty Solicitor and Higher Court Advocate. Revised by Robert Cashman on 28th November 2025.© 2026 PoliceStationRepUK.com. All rights reserved.
